Elephants are indeed endangered, so maybe there's some sarcasm here if the person is being overly nice while actually implying something else. But I'm not sure yet. Option B is similar: "You're welcome my dear friend. Always glad to feed the trolls, that woefully endangered species." Here, instead of elephants, it's trolls. "Trolls" can refer to people who post provocative or offensive messages online to provoke others. The phrase "woefully endangered species" is used sarcastically here because trolls aren't actually endangered; in fact, they are often numerous on the internet. In option A, the mention of elephants being an endangered species is factual, so if the speaker is genuinely happy to help, it's not sarcastic. In option B, the use of "trolls" is key. Trolls are not endangered; they are common. By calling them "woefully endangered species," the speaker is being sarcastic because they're saying the opposite of the truth.
